info.title: CubePath API
info.description: Welcome to CubePath Cloud! Our API helps you manage your cloud infrastructure with ease. Create servers, manage networks, handle payments, and more - all through simple API calls.

post.description: Store your public SSH key for secure, password-free access to your servers.

get.description: View all your stored SSH keys that can be used when creating new servers.

delete.description: Remove an SSH key from your account. Keys in use by servers cannot be deleted.

put.description: Update the name of an existing SSH key.

get.description: View all floating IP addresses assigned to your organization. Manage public IPs for high availability and failover.

post.description: Acquire an available floating IP from the pool. The IP will be reserved for your organization and can then be assigned to a VPS or Baremetal server.
Release a floating IP back to the pool. If the IP is currently assigned to a server, it will be automatically unassigned first.
Configure reverse DNS (PTR record) for a floating IP address. Pass empty reverse_dns to delete.

parameters.name: ip

schema.description: Floating IP address to configure reverse DNS for
schema.title: Ip

parameters.description: Floating IP address to configure reverse DNS for

parameters.name: reverse_dns

schema.description: Reverse DNS hostname (e.g., 'myhost.example.com'). Leave empty to delete.
schema.title: Reverse Dns

parameters.description: Reverse DNS hostname (e.g., 'myhost.example.com'). Leave empty to delete.

get.description: View all your projects with complete details including servers, networks, and bare metal resources.

schema.title: Response Get Projects By User Organization Projects  Get

post.description: Organize your resources into projects for better management and cost tracking across teams and applications.

put.description: Update project details such as name.

delete.description: Remove a project when no longer needed. All servers must be deleted first.

post.description: Set up an isolated private network for secure communication between your servers. Perfect for multi-tier applications.

put.description: Modify network name and label to better organize your infrastructure.

delete.description: Remove a private network when no longer needed. All servers must be disconnected first.

post.description: Move a private network from its current project to a different project within the same organization.

get.description: View all custom routes configured for a private network. Each route forwards traffic to a specific IP, server, or baremetal.

schema.title: Response List Network Routes Networks  Network Id  Routes Get

post.description: Add a custom route that directs traffic for a CIDR through a target IP, server, or baremetal in your network.

delete.description: Remove a custom route from a private network.

get.description: View the dynamic routing (BGP) sessions configured on a private network. Each peer exchanges routes with one of your servers.

schema.title: Response List Network Bgp Peers Networks  Network Id  Bgp Peers Get

post.description: Set up a dynamic routing (BGP) session so a server in your network can exchange routes automatically with the network gateway.

patch.description: Adjust a dynamic routing (BGP) session — change its route limit, description, or enable/disable it.

delete.description: Remove a dynamic routing (BGP) session from a private network.

get.description: Get all VPS servers in your organization with complete details including specifications, status, and network configuration.

post.description: Deploy a new VPS server with your chosen plan, operating system, and location. Your server will be ready in minutes.
Permanently destroy a VPS server and release all associated resources. This action cannot be undone.
Start, stop, restart, or reset your server. Use power controls to manage server uptime and maintenance.
Change your server's resources by switching to a different plan. Upgrade for more power or downgrade to save costs.

get.description: Access your server's console through VNC for troubleshooting and emergency access when SSH is unavailable.

post.description: Wipe and reinstall your server with a fresh operating system. All data will be erased during reinstallation.
Change the root/administrator password for your server. New password takes effect after a few minutes.

patch.description: Modify server label and hostname for better organization and identification of your resources.

get.description: Monitor your server's performance with real-time and historical metrics for CPU, memory, disk, and network usage.
Get total bandwidth usage for the current month
Get all available VPS plans organized by location and cluster.

schema.title: Response Get Vps Plans Vps Plans Get

get.description: Browse available operating systems and applications for VPS deployment.

schema.title: Response Get Vps Templates Vps Templates Get

get.description: Retrieve automatic backup configuration for your server including schedule and retention settings.

put.description: Configure automatic backup schedule, retention period, and maximum number of backups to keep.

get.description: View all backups for your server including manual and automatic backups with their status and creation dates.

post.description: Create an immediate backup of your server. Manual backups are useful before major changes or maintenance.
Restore your server to a previous state from a backup. This will overwrite all current data on the server.

delete.description: Permanently delete a backup to free up storage space. This action cannot be undone.

post.description: Move a VPS server from its current project to a different project within the same organization.
Add one or more SSH keys to an existing VPS

delete.description: Remove an SSH key from an existing VPS

post.description: Enable or disable destruction protection on your server. When enabled, the server cannot be destroyed or reinstalled until protection is removed.

get.summary: List availability groups for a project
get.description: Get all availability groups for a specific project.
Get details of a specific availability group by UUID.

delete.description: Delete an availability group. Can only delete groups with no VPS assigned.

post.description: Create a new availability group to organize and optimize your VPS server placement.
Assign a VPS to an availability group

delete.description: Remove a VPS from its availability group

get.description: View resource usage for a group of servers

schema.title: Response Get Availability Group Metrics Vps Availability Groups  Group Uuid  Metrics Get

post.summary: Move availability group to another project
post.description: Move an availability group from its current project to a different project within the same organization.

get.description: Get available ISOs with mount status for a specific VPS.

post.description: Attach an ISO to a VPS.

delete.description: Detach ISO from a VPS.

post.description: Attach a private network to a VPS. The server must be restarted for changes to take effect.

delete.description: Detach a private network from a VPS. The server must be restarted for changes to take effect.

get.description: Get all baremetal servers in your organization with complete details including specifications, status, and network configuration.
List available operating systems for baremetal server
Get all available baremetal server models organized by location with specifications and pricing.

post.description: Deploy a baremetal server to a project.
Start, stop or restart a baremetal server

put.description:
Enable or disable monitoring for a baremetal server.

Parameters:
- enable: true to enable monitoring, false to disable

post.description: Reinstall the operating system on a baremetal server with a specified OS and disk layout configuration.

delete.description: Cancel an ongoing reinstallation task for a baremetal server.

post.description: Boot the baremetal server into rescue mode for system recovery and maintenance.
Reset the server's BMC (Baseboard Management Controller). The BMC will be unavailable for 1-2 minutes.

get.description: Get total bandwidth usage for the last 30 days
Monitor your baremetal server's network performance with real-time and historical metrics for interface traffic.
Get hardware monitoring data from the server's BMC including temperatures, fans, voltages, and power metrics.

post.description: Enable or disable destruction protection on your baremetal server. When enabled, the operating system cannot be reinstalled until protection is removed.
Move a baremetal server from its current project to a different project within the same organization.
Add SSH keys to a baremetal server.

delete.description: Remove an SSH key from a baremetal server.

post.description: Attach a private network to a baremetal server. The VLAN is added to the trunk on all assigned switch ports.

delete.description: Detach the private network from a baremetal server. The VLAN is removed from the trunk on all assigned switch ports.

get.description: Endpoint to retrieve DDoS attacks from external API.
Endpoint to retrieve detailed information about a specific DDoS attack from external API.

Endpoint to retrieve traffic graph data for a specific DDoS attack.
Returns time-series data with traffic metrics.
Get all maintenances - public endpoint

parameters.name: hide_ended

schema.description: Hide maintenances that have already ended
schema.title: Hide Ended

parameters.description: Hide maintenances that have already ended

schema.title: Response Get Maintenances Status Maintenances Get

get.description: Get all incidents - public endpoint

schema.title: Response Get Incidents Status Incidents Get

get.description: List all tunnels for the current organization with complete information.
List all firewall groups for the current user's projects

schema.title: Response List Firewall Groups Firewall Groups Get

post.description: Create a new firewall group for a project

put.description: Update a firewall group and trigger sync for all VPS using it

delete.description: Delete a firewall group only if not in use by any resources

put.description: Replace all firewall groups assigned to a VPS and trigger sync

get.description: Export Ansible inventory file with all VPS and Baremetal servers
View your notification channels (email, Slack, Discord) that Cloud Alerts can send to.

schema.title: Response List Notificators Triggers Notificators  Get

post.description: Add a notification channel (email, Slack, or Discord) that Cloud Alerts can send notifications to.

get.description: View a single notification channel.

put.description: Change a notification channel's name, settings, or enabled state.

delete.description: Remove a notification channel. Alerts that use it will no longer send to it.

get.description: View all your Cloud Alerts and the current status of each one.

post.description: Create a Cloud Alert that watches a metric on a server and notifies you when it crosses your threshold.

get.description: View a single Cloud Alert, including its condition, target, and actions.

put.description: Change an alert's condition, threshold, target, or actions.

delete.description: Remove a Cloud Alert. It stops watching and firing immediately.

get.description: See when an alert fired and recovered, with the measured values at each event.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of history records to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of history records to return

schema.title: Response Get Trigger History Triggers  Trigger Id  History Get

get.description: View the regions you can point a DNS record to. Choose a region to serve different answers to visitors near it, or 'global' to serve the same answer everywhere.

schema.title: Response List Dns Regions Dns Regions Get

get.description: List all DNS zones for the organization.

schema.title: Response List Dns Zones Dns Zones Get

post.description: Create a new empty DNS zone.

Create a new DNS zone and import records from a BIND zone file.

Creates the zone and imports records in one operation.
Records are stored immediately and will sync to PowerDNS once the zone is verified.

Create a new DNS zone and import existing DNS records via scanning.

Scans public DNS servers to discover existing records for the domain.
Records are stored immediately and will sync to PowerDNS once the zone is verified.
Useful for migrating from another DNS provider.

get.description: Get a specific DNS zone.

delete.description: Delete a DNS zone.

post.description:
Request domain verification by checking NS records.

Verifies that the domain's NS records point to CubePath nameservers.
Creates an async task to verify. Check zone status to see result.

Import DNS records from a BIND-compatible zone file to an existing zone.

Parses the uploaded zone file and creates DNS records.
Skips SOA, NS records and records that already exist.
Records will sync to PowerDNS once the zone is verified.

Scan existing DNS records for the domain and optionally import them.

Queries public DNS servers to discover existing records for the domain.
Useful for migrating from another DNS provider.

If auto_import is True, records are automatically created.
If False, only returns the discovered records without importing.
Records will sync to PowerDNS once the zone is verified.

get.description: List all records in a DNS zone.

schema.title: Response List Dns Records Dns Zones  Zone Uuid  Records Get

post.description: Create a new DNS record.

delete.description: Delete a DNS record.

get.description: View all health checks in a DNS zone and whether each target is currently healthy.

schema.title: Response List Zone Health Checks Dns Zones  Zone Uuid  Health Checks Get

get.description: View the health check attached to a DNS record, including its current status.

put.description: Add or update the health check on a DNS record. When the target stops responding, the record is automatically removed from DNS answers until it recovers. Billed at $10/month per health check.

delete.description: Remove the health check from a DNS record. Billing for it stops immediately.

get.description: Get SOA settings for a DNS zone.

put.description:
Update SOA settings for a DNS zone.

Changes will sync to PowerDNS. The serial number is automatically incremented.

post.summary: Move DNS zone to another project
post.description: Move a DNS zone to a different project within the same organization.

get.description:
List available Load Balancer plans grouped by location.

Returns all locations with their available LB plans.
List all Load Balancers with full details (listeners, targets, health checks).

post.description: Create a new Load Balancer.

delete.description: Delete a Load Balancer.

post.description: Resize a Load Balancer to a different plan.

get.description: View your load balancer's bandwidth consumption for the current billing month.
Monitor your load balancer's performance with real-time and historical metrics for connections and network usage.

parameters.name: metrics

schema.description: Comma-separated metrics: active_connections, total_connections, bytes_in, bytes_out
schema.title: Metrics

parameters.description: Comma-separated metrics: active_connections, total_connections, bytes_in, bytes_out

delete.description: Delete a listener.

post.description: Add a target to a listener.
Add multiple targets to a listener in a single operation.

delete.description: Remove a target from a listener.

post.description: Set a target to draining mode (graceful removal).

post.summary: Move load balancer to another project
post.description: Move a load balancer from its current project to a different project within the same organization.

get.description: List available NAT Gateway plans grouped by location.
List all NAT Gateways belonging to the user's organization.

post.description: Create a NAT Gateway. An existing private network is required (passed by UUID) and the gateway is allocated a public IPv4 and IPv6 floating IP from the network's location. Both IPs must be available — the request is rejected if either is exhausted.

get.description: Get a NAT Gateway by UUID.

patch.description: Update name/label of a NAT Gateway.

delete.description: Delete a NAT Gateway. Releases floating IPs and queues VM destruction.

post.description: Move a NAT Gateway to a different plan (within the same cluster).

post.summary: Move NAT Gateway to another project
post.description:
Move the NAT Gateway to another project within the same organization.

The destination project must be in the same organization and the underlying
private network must already belong to the destination project.
Enable or disable destruction protection on your NAT Gateway. When enabled, the gateway cannot be deleted until protection is removed.

get.description: Monitor your NAT Gateway's network usage with real-time and historical bandwidth metrics.
Total inbound + outbound bytes through the gateway during the current calendar month.
List available CDN plans.
List all CDN zones in the organization.

post.description: Create a new CDN zone.

get.description: Get a specific CDN zone with all details.

patch.description:
Update a CDN zone.

SSL options:
- ssl_type='automatic' (default): Automatically provisions Let's Encrypt certificate
- ssl_type='custom' + certificate_uuid: Uses an uploaded certificate

Returns cname_target that the user must configure in their DNS.

delete.description: Delete a CDN zone.

patch.description: Update an origin. You can provide a full origin_url to update address, port, protocol, host_header, and base_path at once.

delete.description: Delete an origin.

get.description: List edge rules for a CDN zone (cache, redirects, headers).

get.description: Get a specific WAF rule.

Get requests per second over time for a CDN zone.

get.summary: Get Bandwidth Over Time or by Region
get.description:
Get bandwidth over time or grouped by region for a CDN zone.

- group_by='time': Returns bandwidth over time (default)
- group_by='region': Returns bandwidth grouped by geographic region
Get cache hit/miss breakdown over time for a CDN zone.
Get HTTP status code distribution for a CDN zone.
Get top URLs by request count for a CDN zone.
Get top countries by request count for a CDN zone.
Get top ASN by request count for a CDN zone.
Get top user agents by request count for a CDN zone.
Get blocked requests breakdown by reason for a CDN zone.
Get requests distribution by PoP for a CDN zone (grouped by location prefix, e.g. BCN01, MIA01).
Get pricing information for a CDN zone including regional bandwidth costs.
Get top file extensions by request count and bandwidth for a CDN zone.

post.summary: Re-request SSL certificate for a CDN zone
post.description: Trigger a new automatic SSL issuance for the zone's current custom domain. Use when the first attempt failed (e.g. the CNAME wasn't configured yet) and you've since fixed the DNS. Only applies to zones with a custom domain and automatic SSL.

post.summary: Move CDN zone to another project
post.description: Move a CDN zone to a different project within the same organization.
Generate a new secret for signed URL validation. Invalidates every existing signed URL immediately. The new secret is returned in this response and cannot be retrieved again — store it securely.
Server-side helper to produce a valid signed URL for a Token Auth zone without having to re-implement the hashing locally. Useful for testing and for backends that prefer to delegate signing.

get.description:
List all IPs for the organization with their DDoS protection status.
Only shows IPs with Premium protection (excludes Basic).
Returns single IPs and subnets separately, with subnets expanded into individual IPs.

parameters.name: ip_type

schema.description: Filter by IP type: IPv4 or IPv6
schema.title: Ip Type

parameters.description: Filter by IP type: IPv4 or IPv6

get.description:
Get the countries assigned to a protection profile for geo-blocking.
Returns empty list if no profile exists (allows configuring countries before profile is created).

put.description:
Update the countries assigned to a protection profile.
Replaces all existing country assignments with the provided list.

get.description:
Get the ASNs assigned to a protection profile for filtering.
Returns empty list if no profile exists (allows configuring ASNs before profile is created).

put.description:
Update the ASNs assigned to a protection profile.
Replaces all existing ASN assignments with the provided list.

get.description:
Get the prefix lists assigned to a protection profile.
Returns empty list if no profile exists.

put.description:
Update the prefix lists assigned to a protection profile.
Replaces all existing prefix list assignments with the provided list.

get.description:
Get the protection profile for a specific IP/network.
Returns default values if no profile exists.
Only available for IPs with Premium protection.

put.description:
Create or update the protection profile for a specific IP/network.
If a subnet is provided (e.g., /24), profiles are created for each individual IP.
Only available for IPs with Premium protection.

delete.description:
Delete the protection profile for a specific IP/network.
If a subnet is provided, deletes profiles for all IPs in the subnet.
This resets the IPs to default protection settings.

get.description:
List all available countries for geo-blocking.
Returns country codes and names that can be used in whitelist/blacklist modes.

List all prefix lists for the organization.
Includes both organization-specific lists and global lists (organization_id = NULL).

post.description:
Create a new custom prefix list for the organization.
Limited to MAX_PREFIX_LISTS_PER_ORG (3) per organization.

delete.description: Delete a prefix list by UUID. Only organization-owned lists can be deleted.

get.description: List all entries (networks) in a prefix list.

schema.title: Response List Prefix List Entries Ddos Mitigation Prefix Lists  Uuid  Entries Get

post.description: Add a network entry to a prefix list.

delete.description: Remove a network entry from a prefix list.

get.description:
List all available ASNs for filtering.
Returns ASN numbers and names that can be used in whitelist/blacklist modes.

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Search by ASN number or name
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Search by ASN number or name

get.description: List all firewall rules for a specific IP/network.

post.description:
Create a new firewall rule for a specific IP/network.

If a subnet is provided (e.g., /24), the rule is expanded and created
for each individual IP in the subnet (XDP requires /32 IPs).

Actions (with protocol restrictions):
- 0: DROP - Drop packets (TCP/UDP/ICMP)
- 1: ACCEPT - Accept packets (TCP/UDP/ICMP)
- 2: FILTER - Continue filtering (TCP/UDP/ICMP)
- 10: FiveM TCP L1 - Monitor only (TCP only)
- 11: FiveM TCP L2 - Require auth (TCP only)
- 12: FiveM TCP L10 - Auth + rate limit (TCP only)
- 15: FiveM UDP L1 - Monitor only (UDP only)
- 16: FiveM UDP L2 - Require auth (UDP only)
- 17: FiveM UDP L3 - Self-auth getinfo (UDP only)
- 20: RDP TCP - Full auth + rate limit (TCP only)
- 21: RDP UDP - Require auth (UDP only)
- 30: DNS UDP - Validate DNS header (UDP only)
- 31: DNS TCP - Validate header + length (TCP only)
- 40: Minecraft Java - Handshake validation (TCP only)
- 50: TLS - TLS record validation (TCP only)

delete.description:
Delete firewall rules for all IPs in a subnet matching the given protocol and port.
Useful for removing rules that were created via subnet expansion.
Delete a firewall rule by its ID.

post.description:
Query traffic capture logs from ClickHouse.

Requires a destination IP that belongs to the user's organization.
Returns packet-level data for DDoS analysis.

get.description:
Get all IPs with Premium DDoS protection for the current user's organization.
These are the IPs that can be queried in traffic capture.

post.description:
Get aggregated traffic statistics for graphing DROP vs PASS over time.
Returns time-bucketed data suitable for charts.

get.description: Browse the marketplace of installable Kubernetes addons.
View detailed information about a specific addon.
View all addons installed on a Kubernetes cluster.

post.description: Install an addon on your Kubernetes cluster. The installation runs in the background.

delete.description: Remove an installed addon from your Kubernetes cluster.

get.description: View all node pools in a Kubernetes cluster with their workers and configuration.

post.description: Add a new node pool to your Kubernetes cluster with the specified plan and worker count.

patch.description: Change the number of workers, labels, or taints for a node pool.

delete.description: Remove a node pool and destroy all its workers. Cannot delete the last pool.

post.description: Add new worker nodes to an existing node pool.

delete.description: Remove a specific worker node from a node pool. The node will be drained and destroyed.

get.description: View available Kubernetes versions for cluster creation.
View server plans that meet the minimum requirements for Kubernetes worker nodes.
View all your Kubernetes clusters with status and node counts.

post.description: Deploy a new managed Kubernetes cluster. The control plane is managed by CubePath and workers run on your VPS instances.

get.description: View detailed information about a Kubernetes cluster including node pools, workers, and network configuration.

patch.description: Update the name or label of a Kubernetes cluster.

delete.description: Permanently delete a Kubernetes cluster. All workers will be destroyed and the control plane removed.

post.description: Enable or disable destruction protection on your Kubernetes cluster. When enabled, the cluster cannot be deleted until protection is removed.

get.description: Download the kubeconfig file to connect to your Kubernetes cluster with kubectl.

post.description: Move a Kubernetes cluster and all its resources to a different project within your organization.

get.summary: List load balancers targeting this cluster
get.description: View all load balancers that have targets pointing to worker nodes of this Kubernetes cluster.
View available managed database plans grouped by location. Prices are per node per hour.

post.description: Scale your database horizontally (change the number of replicas) or vertically (move to a bigger or smaller plan). Provide either 'replicas' or 'plan_uuid'.

get.description: Retrieve the host, port, username, password and ready-to-use connection URI for your database. Keep these credentials secret.

post.description: Generate a new admin password for your database. Applications using the old password will need to be updated once the rotation completes.

get.description: View the tunable configuration parameters of your database: defaults, allowed ranges and whether changing them requires a rolling restart.

patch.description: Change tunable configuration parameters of your database. Most changes apply without downtime; some parameters require a brief rolling restart.

get.description: Monitor your database with real-time and historical metrics: connections, CPU, memory and replication lag.

parameters.name: metrics

schema.description: Comma-separated metrics: connections, cpu, memory, replication_lag
schema.title: Metrics

parameters.description: Comma-separated metrics: connections, cpu, memory, replication_lag

get.description: View the logical databases of a Managed Database instance.

post.description: Create a logical database inside a Managed Database instance. Logical databases are managed objects: the platform creates them and re-applies them after every lifecycle event. Not applicable to Valkey (numbered databases).

delete.description: Delete a logical database from a Managed Database instance. The data is destroyed.

get.description: View the users of a Managed Database instance. Passwords are never returned here.

post.description: Create a user inside a Managed Database instance. Users are managed objects: the platform creates them and re-applies them after every lifecycle event. If no password is provided, a secure one is generated. The password is returned ONCE in this response and can never be retrieved again — store it now.

delete.description: Delete a user from a Managed Database instance.

get.description: View all your managed databases with engine, status and connection endpoint.

post.description: Deploy a new fully managed database (MySQL, Valkey or PostgreSQL) with high availability, automatic failover and a public connection endpoint. CubePath manages all the underlying infrastructure for you.

get.description: View detailed information about a managed database: engine, plan, replicas and connection endpoint.

patch.description: Update the name, label or backup policy of a managed database.

delete.description: Permanently delete a managed database and all its data. This action cannot be undone — make sure you have exported any data you need.

post.description: Enable or disable destruction protection on your managed database. When enabled, the database cannot be deleted until protection is removed.

origin_url.title: Origin Url
origin_url.description: Full origin URL (e.g., https://s3.example.com/bucket-name/). If provided, extracts address, port, protocol, host_header, and base_path automatically.

host_header.title: Host Header
host_header.description: Custom Host header to send to origin

origin_url.title: Origin Url
origin_url.description: Full origin URL. If provided, extracts address, port, protocol, host_header, and base_path.

client_ip.title: Client Ip
client_ip.description: Required when the zone has token_auth_ip_binding enabled. The signature includes this IP.

CDNTokenSignURLRequest.title: CDNTokenSignURLRequest
CDNTokenSignURLRequest.description: Generate a signed URL for a Token Auth-enabled zone.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (uses default if not specified)

ssl_type.title: Ssl Type
ssl_type.description: SSL type: automatic (Let's Encrypt) or custom

token_auth_enabled.title: Token Auth Enabled
token_auth_enabled.description: Enable signed URL validation. Activating without a previously-set secret auto-generates one; the secret is returned in the response only on first generation. Use POST /cdn/zones/{uuid}/token-auth/rotate-secret to rotate.

token_auth_ip_binding.title: Token Auth Ip Binding
token_auth_ip_binding.description: Bind signed URLs to the client IP. Tightens security against URL sharing but breaks for clients behind CGNAT or with rotating IPs.

cors_enabled.title: Cors Enabled
cors_enabled.description: Enable CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) headers for this zone. Required when your content is fetched from JavaScript on another domain (HLS players, fetch APIs, etc.).

cors_allow_origins.title: Cors Allow Origins
cors_allow_origins.description: Origins allowed to fetch this zone's content. Use "*" to allow any origin, or a comma-separated list of exact origins (scheme + host + optional port). Ignored when cors_enabled is false.

iso_codes.title: Iso Codes
iso_codes.description: List of country ISO codes to assign

DNSHealthCheckOut.title: DNSHealthCheckOut
DNSHealthCheckOut.description:
Health check response. A failing check pulls its record's value from the
answer set at every PoP that observes the failure.

target.title: Target
target.description: Host/IP to probe; defaults to the record's own value

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether the check is active (billed while enabled)

DNSHealthCheckUpsert.title: DNSHealthCheckUpsert
DNSHealthCheckUpsert.description: Create or update the health check attached to a DNS record (1 per record).

name.title: Name
name.description: Record name (@ for apex, or subdomain)

DNSRegionOut.title: DNSRegionOut
DNSRegionOut.description: A GeoDNS region option for the region selector.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID to associate the zone with

DNSZoneSOAUpdate.title: DNSZoneSOAUpdate
DNSZoneSOAUpdate.description: Update SOA settings for a DNS zone

FirewallRulesListResponse.title: FirewallRulesListResponse

LBHealthCheckUpdate.title: LBHealthCheckUpdate
LBHealthCheckUpdate.description: Create or update a health check

ssl_mode.title: Ssl Mode
ssl_mode.description: SSL mode (auto-detected if certificate is provided)

LBTargetBatchCreate.title: LBTargetBatchCreate
LBTargetBatchCreate.description: Add multiple targets to a listener in a single operation

target_uuid.title: Target Uuid
target_uuid.description: UUID of VPS, Baremetal ID, or Availability Group UUID

LBTargetCreate.title: LBTargetCreate
LBTargetCreate.description: Add a target to a listener

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (uses default project if not specified)

network_id.title: Network Id
network_id.description: Optional private network to attach the LB to. When set, the LB gets a private NIC on this network and reaches targets by their private IP on the same network (strict — no public fallback). Must be in the same location as the LB.

LoadBalancerResize.title: LoadBalancerResize
LoadBalancerResize.description: Resize a Load Balancer to a different plan

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Target project ID to move the resource to

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID (inferred from the network if not specified)

NatGatewayMoveProject.title: NatGatewayMoveProject
NatGatewayMoveProject.description: Move a NAT Gateway to another project

NatGatewayResize.title: NatGatewayResize
NatGatewayResize.description: Resize a NAT Gateway to a different plan

OrganizationIPsOut.title: OrganizationIPsOut
OrganizationIPsOut.description: Schema para la respuesta completa de IPs de la organización

uuids.title: Uuids
uuids.description: List of prefix list UUIDs to assign

PrefixListAssignmentUpdate.title: PrefixListAssignmentUpdate
PrefixListAssignmentUpdate.description: Schema for updating prefix list assignments.

destination_ip.title: Destination Ip
destination_ip.description: Required: IP or subnet to filter (must belong to user)

TrafficStatsBucket.title: TrafficStatsBucket
TrafficStatsBucket.description: Single time bucket for traffic stats.

destination_ips.title: Destination Ips
destination_ips.description: IPs to filter (must belong to user). Empty = all user IPs

password.title: Password
password.description: New password must be at least 8 characters long.

ssh_key_ids.title: Ssh Key Ids
ssh_key_ids.description: List of SSH key IDs to add to the VPS

password.title: Password
password.description: If empty, no root password is set. Minimum 8 characters if provided.

ipv4.title: Ipv4
ipv4.description: IPv4 address (dual-stack). Additional $1.50/month. IPv6 is always included for free.

ipv6.title: Ipv6
ipv6.description: Public IPv6 address (free). Disable to deploy without any public IP — requires a private network.

custom_cloudinit.title: Custom Cloudinit
custom_cloudinit.description: Custom cloud-init configuration (YAML). Add your own packages, runcmd, write_files, etc. Only for Linux templates.

firewall_group_ids.title: Firewall Group Ids
firewall_group_ids.description: Firewall group ID to assign to this VPS (max 1)

enable_backups.title: Enable Backups
enable_backups.description: Enable automatic backups for this VPS

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Environment configuration values (e.g. API keys). Transient, not stored.

password.title: Password
password.description: New password for the reinstalled VPS. Must be at least 8 characters long.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Environment variables for app templates (e.g. API keys, license keys)
